devanagari: सत्राजितं हत्वा शतधन्वनः स्यमन्तकहरणं तस्य च वधः अक्रूरस्य च पलायनं पुनर्द्वारकायां आगमनं च । श्रीबादरायणिरुवाच । विज्ञातार्थोऽपि गोविन...
english_devnagari: satrājitaṃ hatvā śatadhanvanaḥ syamantakaharaṇaṃ tasya ca vadhaḥ akrūrasya ca palāyanaṃ punardvārakāyāṃ āgamanaṃ ca śrībādarāyaṇiruvāca vijñātārtho kuntīṃ ca kulyakaraṇe saharāmo yayau kurūn

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The ruler of the world cleared off the accusation levelled against Him and explained the details of the case to Balarāma who had been away so long and had returned for seeing the gem (and to convince himself personally).

devanagari: भीष्मं कृपं स विदुरं गान्धारीं द्रोणमेव च । तुल्यदुःखौ च सङ्गम्य हा कष्टमिति होचतुः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: bhīṣmaṃ kṛpaṃ sa viduraṃ gāndhārīṃ droṇameva ca tulyaduḥkhau ca saṅgamya hā kaṣṭamiti hocatuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kaṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kaṣ class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ kaś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kaś class 1 verb]

translation: But on seeing it, he regarded himself to be the proper person, to possess it and coveted to have it, Oh prominent Kaurava. Satyabhāmā considered herself to be (legally) the proper heir eligible (to possess) that hereditary property.

devanagari: लब्ध्वैतदन्तरं राजन्शतधन्वानमूचतुः । अक्रूरकृतवर्माणौ मणिः कस्मान्न गृह्यते ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: labdhvaitadantaraṃ rājanśatadhanvānamūcatuḥ akrūrakṛtavarmāṇau maṇiḥ kasmānna gṛhyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Without knowing the mind of Hari, she confidently wished to possess it. Even Jāmbavatī desired to get that gem as it was given as dowry by her father (as such her claim was legal).

devanagari: योऽस्मभ्यं सम्प्रतिश्रुत्य कन्यारत्नं विगर्ह्य नः । कृष्णायादान्न सत्राजित्कस्माद्भ्रातरमन्वियात् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: yo kṛṣṇāyādānna satrājitkasmādbhrātaramanviyāt
translation: Knowing all these (covetous) claims, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the consort of goddess Lakṣmī handed over again the gem to Akrūra.

devanagari: एवं भिन्नमतिस्ताभ्यां सत्राजितमसत्तमः । शयानमवधील्लोभात्स पापः क्षीणजीवितः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ bhinnamatistābhyāṃ satrājitamasattamaḥ śayānamavadhīllobhātsa pāpaḥ kṣīṇajīvitaḥ
translation: His mind being thus thoroughly perverted and prejudiced, the wicked-most, sinful fellow with his span of life diminished (and was about to be terminated soon), murdered Satrājit out of greed, while he was asleep.

devanagari: स्त्रीणां विक्रोशमानानां क्रन्दन्तीनामनाथवत् । हत्वा पशून्सौनिकवन्मणिमादाय जग्मिवान् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: strīṇāṃ vikrośamānānāṃ krandantīnāmanāthavat hatvā paśūnsaunikavanmaṇimādāya jagmivān
translation: While the women (in the family) were screaming and wailing loudly and helplessly, he, like a butcher knifing a beast, finished with Satrājit and absconded, taking with him the gem Syamantaka.

devanagari: सत्यभामा च पितरं हतं वीक्ष्य शुचार्पिता । व्यलपत्तात तातेति हा हतास्मीति मुह्यती ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: satyabhāmā ca pitaraṃ hataṃ vīkṣya śucārpitā vyalapattāta tāteti hā hatāsmīti muhyatī

translation: Beholding her father thus slain, Satyabhāmā was stricken with grief and bewailed, ‘Oh father! father I am undone’ And she fainted.

devanagari: तैलद्रोण्यां मृतं प्रास्य जगाम गजसाह्वयम् । कृष्णाय विदितार्थाय तप्ताऽचख्यौ पितुर्वधम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tailadroṇyāṃ mṛtaṃ prāsya jagāma gajasāhvayam kṛṣṇāya viditārthāya taptā

translation: Depositing that dead body in a trough full of oil (to prevent its decomposition), she rushed to Hastināpura. Tormented as she was, she reported the matter of her father’s assassination to Kṛṣṇa who (being omniscient) already knew the facts (of the case).

devanagari: तदाकर्ण्येश्वरौ राजन्ननुसृत्य नृलोकताम् । अहो नः परमं कष्टमित्यस्राक्षौ विलेपतुः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tadākarṇyeśvarau rājannanusṛtya nṛlokatām aho naḥ paramaṃ kaṣṭamityasrākṣau vilepatuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kaṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kaṣ class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ kaś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kaś class 1 verb]

translation: Hearing that (tragic incident) Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa, both the Lords.of the world, following the (customary) way of human beings bewailed, ‘Alas, what a calamity has befallen us!’, with eyes flowing with tears.

devanagari: आगत्य भगवांस्तस्मात्सभार्यः साग्रजः पुरम् । शतधन्वानमारेभे हन्तुं हर्तुं मणिं ततः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: āgatya bhagavāṃstasmātsabhāryaḥ sāgrajaḥ puram śatadhanvānamārebhe hantuṃ hartuṃ maṇiṃ tataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: 4(A). And hastened back to Dvārakā to slay Śatadhanvā.

devanagari: सोऽपि कृतोद्यमं ज्ञात्वा भीतः प्राणपरीप्सया । साहाय्ये कृतवर्माणमयाचत स चाब्रवीत् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: so sāhāyye kṛtavarmāṇamayācata sa cābravīt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[imperative active second plural], [injunctive middle third single]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: Knowing the punitive measures adopted by Kṛṣṇa, Śatadhanvā got afraid and being anxious to save his life, he begged of Kṛtavarmā’s help. But he (Kṛtavarmā) replied:

devanagari: नाहमीश्वरयोः कुर्यां हेलनं रामकृष्णयोः । को नु क्षेमाय कल्पेत तयोर्वृजिनमाचरन् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: nāhamīśvarayoḥ kuryāṃ helanaṃ rāmakṛṣṇayoḥ ko nu kṣemāya kalpeta tayorvṛjinamācaran
translation: “I will not offend Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa, the Lords of the universe. Who can hope to live in peace after committing an offence against them?

devanagari: कंसः सहानुगोऽपीतो यद्द्वेषात्त्याजितः श्रिया । जरासन्धः सप्तदश संयुगाद्विरथो गतः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṃsaḥ sahānugo jarāsandhaḥ saptadaśa saṃyugādviratho gataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The great warrior Kaṃsa was forsaken by the goddess of wealth and prosperity due to his hatred of them, and had to leave this world along with his followers, while Jarāsandha had to take to flight in (each of the) seventeen expeditions losing even his own chariot”

devanagari: प्रत्याख्यातः स चाक्रूरं पार्ष्णिग्राहमयाचत । सोऽप्याह को विरुध्येत विद्वानीश्वरयोर्बलम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: pratyākhyātaḥ sa cākrūraṃ pārṣṇigrāhamayācata so

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation:
Being thus refused help (by Kṛtavarmā), he entreated Akrūra for help. But he too replied: “What person knowing the might of these two Lords of the world, can dare to oppose them?
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text adds:]

devanagari: य इदं लीलया विश्वं सृजत्यवति हन्ति च । चेष्टां विश्वसृजो यस्य न विदुर्मोहिताजया ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: ya idaṃ līlayā viśvaṃ sṛjatyavati hanti ca ceṣṭāṃ viśvasṛjo yasya na vidurmohitājayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [locative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation: A. God Siva whose abode is mount Kailāsa, used to bow down to Hari with his head He (Hari) has vanquished Hamsa and Ḍimbhaka who have become arrogant and overbearing due to boons (got by them), in the battle. The great poisonous serpent (Kāliya) was expelled by him from the pool (in the Yamunā).

devanagari: यः सप्तहायनः शैलमुत्पाट्यैकेन पाणिना । दधार लीलया बाल उच्छिलीन्ध्रमिवार्भकः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: yaḥ saptahāyanaḥ śailamutpāṭyaikena pāṇinā dadhāra līlayā bāla ucchilīndhramivārbhakaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: It is out of sport that he creates, maintains and destroys this Universe. People deluded by his Māyā (the Unborn) do not comprehend the activities of the Creator of the Universe.

devanagari: नमस्तस्मै भगवते कृष्णायाद्भुतकर्मणे । अनन्तायादिभूताय कूटस्थायात्मने नमः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: namastasmai bhagavate kṛṣṇāyādbhutakarmaṇe anantāyādibhūtāya kūṭasthāyātmane namaḥ
translation: As a boy of seven years, he uprooted a mountain and held it up on one hand easily, as in play, like a child holding an umbrella-like mushroom.

devanagari: प्रत्याख्यातः स तेनापि शतधन्वा महामणिम् । तस्मिन्न्यस्याश्वमारुह्य शतयोजनगं ययौ ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: pratyākhyātaḥ sa tenāpi śatadhanvā mahāmaṇim tasminnyasyāśvamāruhya śatayojanagaṃ yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Salutations to the Glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa of miraculous deeds. I bow down to the Infinite Lord, the Prime Cause of the universe, the Indwelling Soul of all.”

devanagari: गरुडध्वजमारुह्य रथं रामजनार्दनौ । अन्वयातां महावेगैरश्वै राजन्गुरुद्रुहम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: garuḍadhvajamāruhya rathaṃ rāmajanārdanau anvayātāṃ mahāvegairaśvai rājangurudruham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Thus refused help by Akrūra, Śatadhanvā deposited the great jewel with him and mounting a horse (or a mare) capable of going hundred yojanas a day, he took to flight.

devanagari: मिथिलायामुपवने विसृज्य पतितं हयम् । पद्भ्यामधावत्सन्त्रस्तः कृष्णोऽप्यन्वद्रवद्रुषा ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: mithilāyāmupavane visṛjya patitaṃ hayam padbhyāmadhāvatsantrastaḥ kṛṣṇo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pat class 1 verb]

translation: Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa rode in their chariots carrying the flag with the emblem of Garuḍa, pursued with very swift horses, the murderer of his father-in-law, Oh king.

devanagari: पदातेर्भगवांस्तस्य पदातिस्तिग्मनेमिना । चक्रेण शिर उत्कृत्य वाससोर्व्यचिनोन्मणिम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: padāterbhagavāṃstasya padātistigmaneminā cakreṇa śira utkṛtya vāsasorvyacinonmaṇim
translation: Śatadhanvā left his horse that dropped down (dead through fatigue), in a park of Mithilā. Being afraid, he began to run on foot, Kṛṣṇa also, out of rage, followed him on foot.

devanagari: अलब्धमणिरागत्य कृष्ण आहाग्रजान्तिकम् । वृथा हतः शतधनुर्मणिस्तत्र न विद्यते ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: alabdhamaṇirāgatya kṛṣṇa āhāgrajāntikam vṛthā hataḥ śatadhanurmaṇistatra na vidyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: The glorious Lord who pursued on foot beheaded him with his Sudarśana disk of very sharp edge, and he searched his upper and lower clothes for the Symantaka jewel.

devanagari: तत आह बलो नूनं स मणिः शतधन्वना । कस्मिंश्चित्पुरुषे न्यस्तस्तमन्वेष पुरं व्रज ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tata āha balo nūnaṃ sa maṇiḥ śatadhanvanā kasmiṃścitpuruṣe nyastastamanveṣa puraṃ vraja

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Not finding the jewel on his person, Kṛṣṇa returned and spoke to his elder brother, “Śatadhanvā has been killed in vain, for the jewel is not on his person.”

devanagari: अहं वैदेहमिच्छामि द्रष्टुं प्रियतमं मम । इत्युक्त्वा मिथिलां राजन्विवेश यदुनन्दनः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ vaidehamicchāmi draṣṭuṃ priyatamaṃ mama ityuktvā mithilāṃ rājanviveśa yadunandanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Then advised Balarama. “The jewel has been certainly deposited by Śatadhanvā with some person (at Dvārakā). You return to the capital and trace him (the person possessing the jewel).

devanagari: तं दृष्ट्वा सहसोत्थाय मैथिलः प्रीतमानसः । अर्हयां आस विधिवदर्हणीयं समर्हणैः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ dṛṣṭvā sahasotthāya maithilaḥ prītamānasaḥ arhayāṃ āsa vidhivadarhaṇīyaṃ samarhaṇaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ arh ], [accusative single from √ arh ]

translation: I wish to see king Videha, my dearest friend.” Speaking thus, Balarāma, the delight of Yadus, entered the city of Mithilā, Oh king!

devanagari: उवास तस्यां कतिचिन्मिथिलायां समा विभुः । मानितः प्रीतियुक्तेन जनकेन महात्मना । ततोऽशिक्षद्गदां काले धार्तराष्ट्रः सुयोधनः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: uvāsa tasyāṃ katicinmithilāyāṃ samā vibhuḥ mānitaḥ prītiyuktena janakena mahātmanā tato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Seeing him come unexpectedly, the king of Mithilā, being highly pleased in mind, immediately rose up to receive him, and worshipped duly his honourable guest with suitable presents and other articles of worship.

devanagari: केशवो द्वारकामेत्य निधनं शतधन्वनः । अप्राप्तिं च मणेः प्राह प्रियायाः प्रियकृद्विभुः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: keśavo dvārakāmetya nidhanaṃ śatadhanvanaḥ aprāptiṃ ca maṇeḥ prāha priyāyāḥ priyakṛdvibhuḥ
translation: The powerful lord Balarāma spent some years in the city of Mithilā. He was duly honoured by the noble-souled Janaka with great affection. It was during that period that Suyodhana, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, learnt the art of fighting with mace, at his feet.

devanagari: ततः स कारयामास क्रिया बन्धोर्हतस्य वै । साकं सुहृद्भिर्भगवान्या याः स्युः साम्परायिकीः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ sa kārayāmāsa kriyā bandhorhatasya vai sākaṃ suhṛdbhirbhagavānyā yāḥ syuḥ sāmparāyikīḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive single from √ han class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ han class 2 verb]
[genitive single from √ han class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ han class 2 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Returning to Dvārakā, Lord Kṛṣṇa who wanted to please his beloved wife, reported the death of Śatadhanvā and also of his not finding the gem with him.

devanagari: अक्रूरः कृतवर्मा च श्रुत्वा शतधनोर्वधम् । व्यूषतुर्भयवित्रस्तौ द्वारकायाः प्रयोजकौ ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: akrūraḥ kṛtavarmā ca śrutvā śatadhanorvadham vyūṣaturbhayavitrastau dvārakāyāḥ prayojakau
translation: Thereupon, along with friends and well-wishers, the Lord caused to be performed all the obsequies of Satrājit his father-in-law who had been murdered.

devanagari: अक्रूरे प्रोषितेऽरिष्टान्यासन्वै द्वारकौकसाम् । शारीरा मानसास्तापा मुहुर्दैविकभौतिकाः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: akrūre proṣite śārīrā mānasāstāpā muhurdaivikabhautikāḥ
translation: Having heard the report of the slaying of Śatadhanvā, Akrūra and Kṛtavarmā, the instigators of the crime, were overwhelmed with fear and absconded (from Dvārakā).

devanagari: इत्यङ्गोपदिशन्त्येके विस्मृत्य प्रागुदाहृतम् । मुनिवासनिवासे किं घटेतारिष्टदर्शनम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: ityaṅgopadiśantyeke vismṛtya prāgudāhṛtam munivāsanivāse kiṃ ghaṭetāriṣṭadarśanam
translation:
When Akrūra left Dvārakā, there appeared evil portents to the citizens of Dvārakā and they frequently suffered from physical and mental troubles as well as troubles caused by gods and elements (Mahābhūtas), so goes the tradition.
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text Adds:]
30-A. Thereupon Śrī Kṛṣṇa inquired of old people of whom Ugrasena was prorñinent: “What is the reason that after such a long time, these ill omens and calamities are befalling us?”

devanagari: देवेऽवर्षति काशीशः श्वफल्कायागताय वै । स्वसुतां गान्दिनीं प्रादात्ततोऽवर्षत्स्म काशिषु ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: deve svasutāṃ gāndinīṃ prādāttato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ prā class 2 verb]

translation: Forgetting what I have spoken before, some chroniclers declare as above. But how is it possible that ominous portents will occur in the home-town of Lord Kṛṣṇa who is the abode of ascetics (capable of counteracting such evils by their very presence)?

devanagari: तत्सुतस्तत्प्रभावोऽसावक्रूरो यत्र यत्र ह । देवोऽभिवर्षते तत्र नोपतापा न मारिकाः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tatsutastatprabhāvo devo
translation: Formerly, so the tradition goes, when the lord of rains did not send seasonal showers, the king of Kāśi gave his daughter Gāndinī in marriage to ŚvaphaLka (the father of Akrūra) who came there as a guest and there fell showers of rain in the kingdom of Kāśi.

devanagari: इति वृद्धवचः श्रुत्वा नैतावदिह कारणम् । इति मत्वा समानाय्य प्राहाक्रूरं जनार्दनः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti vṛddhavacaḥ śrutvā naitāvadiha kāraṇam iti matvā samānāyya prāhākrūraṃ janārdanaḥ
translation: His son Akrūra possesses the same (hereditory) miraculous powers. Wherever he stays, the rain-god sends showers at the proper season. No calamities or epidemics (like cholera) affect at that area.

devanagari: पूजयित्वाभिभाष्यैनं कथयित्वा प्रियाः कथाः । विज्ञताखिलचित्तज्ञः स्मयमान उवाच ह ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: pūjayitvābhibhāṣyainaṃ kathayitvā priyāḥ kathāḥ vijñatākhilacittajñaḥ smayamāna uvāca ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Hearing the advice of the elders and considering that this (i.e. the departure of Akrūra) could be the cause (of these calamities, but the absence of the Syamantaka gem is the real cause), Śrī Kṛṣṇa managed to bring back Akrūra (to Dvārakā) and spoke to him.

devanagari: ननु दानपते न्यस्तस्त्वय्यास्ते शतधन्वना । स्यमन्तको मणिः श्रीमान्विदितः पूर्वमेव नः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: nanu dānapate nyastastvayyāste śatadhanvanā syamantako maṇiḥ śrīmānviditaḥ pūrvameva naḥ
translation: Śrī Kṛṣṇa who knew the minds of all (understood that this great devotee does not covet to possess the Syamantaka gem, and must have certainly brought it with him) received him with deep respect, engaged him in delightful conversation, narrated to him some pleasantries and spoke with a smile:

devanagari: सत्राजितोऽनपत्यत्वाद्गृह्णीयुर्दुहितुः सुताः । दायं निनीयापः पिण्डान्विमुच्यर्णं च शेषितम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: satrājito dāyaṃ ninīyāpaḥ piṇḍānvimucyarṇaṃ ca śeṣitam

[nominative single from √ śiṣ ], [accusative single from √ śiṣ ]

translation: “We already know that the brilliant gem Symantaka is in your possession since it was deposited with you by Śatadhanvā, Oh master of charities.

devanagari: तथापि दुर्धरस्त्वन्यैस्त्वय्यास्तां सुव्रते मणिः । किन्तु मामग्रजः सम्यङ्न प्रत्येति मणिं प्रति ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: tathāpi durdharastvanyaistvayyāstāṃ suvrate maṇiḥ kintu māmagrajaḥ samyaṅna pratyeti maṇiṃ prati
translation: As Satrājita had no male issue.. (legally) the sons of his daughter Satyabhāmā should offer water oblations and lumps of rice (to the departed soul), pay off his debts and inherit the remaining property.

devanagari: दर्शयस्व महाभाग बन्धूनां शान्तिमावह । अव्युच्छिन्ना मखास्तेऽद्य वर्तन्ते रुक्मवेदयः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: darśayasva mahābhāga bandhūnāṃ śāntimāvaha avyucchinnā makhāste
translation: As you observe vows properly, let the Syamantaka gem, however, remain in your possession, as it is difficult for others to retain it. But my elder brother (Balarāma) does not believe that I do not possess the gem (he suspects that I have it with me).

devanagari: एवं सामभिरालब्धः श्वफल्कतनयो मणिम् । आदाय वाससाच्छन्नः ददौ सूर्यसमप्रभम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sāmabhirālabdhaḥ śvaphalkatanayo maṇim ādāya vāsasācchannaḥ dadau sūryasamaprabham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Please show to our relatives (the Symantaka gem) and bring peace of mind to them, Oh highly fortunate Akrūra. (You cannot deny the possession of the gem as) now-a-days your sacrificial performances on altars of gold are going on continuously (without any interruption or break).”

devanagari: स्यमन्तकं दर्शयित्वा ज्ञातिभ्यो रज आत्मनः । विमृज्य मणिना भूयस्तस्मै प्रत्यर्पयत्प्रभुः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: syamantakaṃ darśayitvā jñātibhyo raja ātmanaḥ vimṛjya maṇinā bhūyastasmai pratyarpayatprabhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ṛ ], [vocative single from √ ṛ ], [accusative single from √ ṛ ]

translation: Knowing all these (covetous) claims, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the consort of goddess Lakṣmī handed over again the gem to Akrūra.

devanagari: यस्त्वेतद्भगवत ईश्वरस्य विष्णोर् । वीर्याढ्यं वृजिनहरं सुमङ्गलं च । आख्यानं पठति शृणोत्यनुस्मरेद्वा । दुष्कीर्तिं दुरितमपोह्य याति शान...
english_devnagari: yastvetadbhagavata īśvarasya viṣṇor vīryāḍhyaṃ vṛjinaharaṃ sumaṅgalaṃ ca ākhyānaṃ paṭhati śṛṇotyanusmaredvā duṣkīrtiṃ duritamapohya yāti śāntim
translation:
Having made Akrūra to show the gem to his relatives and clansmen*, he wiped out the blame and suspicion imputed to him by (producing) the gem (from Akrūra) and the Lord returned that gem to Akrūra.
[Here Vj’s Text adds:] {after the *}

devanagari: अथ सप्तपञ्चाशत्तमोऽध्यायः 10. ॥ 57 ॥
english_devnagari: atha saptapañcāśattamo
